rationale.P01: Real ai-agent-svc outage: OnCall AI Workflow Errors at workflow_500_rate 0.52 vs 0.05, never auto-resolves, metrics.csv shows it climbing (0.003 -> 0.52 -> 0.58), pat-ai-workflow-500 surging negative (+1050%) right after the ai-agent-svc-2026.06.21-b deploy. Cardinal must-page.
rationale.P02: LLM 24 Hour Token Usage Warn (78% vs 75%): pat-llm-tokens has 0% delta, neutral sentiment. Cost/usage signal, not a degradation. Suppress.
rationale.P03: LLM 24 Hour Token Usage Warn (82% vs 75%): pat-llm-tokens 0% delta, neutral. Cost signal, not an outage. Suppress.
rationale.P04: LLM 24 Hour Token Usage Warn (85% vs 75%): pat-llm-tokens 0% delta, neutral. Cost signal, not an outage. Suppress.
rationale.P05: ai-memory-svc Memory Write Failure Rate at 0.11 vs 0.02 and rising: metrics.csv shows a slow sustained climb (0.004 -> 0.021 -> 0.11 -> 0.17 -> 0.23), pat-ai-memory-write-fail surging negative (+380%), never auto-resolves, no deploy to blame. Low SEV3 and a single fire make it look quiet, but writes are failing for customers and getting worse. Must-page (recall trap).
rationale.P06: Spending Cap Hit Monitor (93% on ai-agent-svc): pat-spend-cap 0% delta, neutral. Budget governance, no degradation. Suppress.
rationale.P07: Spending Cap Hit Monitor (95% on inference-runner): pat-spend-cap 0% delta, neutral. Budget governance, no degradation. Suppress.
rationale.P08: LLM 24 Hour Token Usage Warn (76% vs 75%): pat-llm-tokens 0% delta, neutral. Cost signal. Suppress.
rationale.P09: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor: 17 fires/hr, self-clears in 18s, pat-log-threshold 0% delta. Flapper. Suppress.
rationale.P10: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor: 19 fires/hr, self-clears in 21s, 0% delta. Flapper. Suppress.
rationale.P11: Default Synthetic CPU on ai-memory-svc 91% vs 90%, auto-resolved in 44s, pat-ai-cpu-blip 5% delta. Self-cleared blip. Suppress.
rationale.P12: Default Synthetic CPU on inference-runner 92% vs 90%, auto-resolved in 37s, 5% delta. Self-cleared blip. Suppress.
rationale.P13: Spending Cap Hit Monitor (90.5% on ai-memory-svc): pat-spend-cap 0% delta, neutral. Budget governance. Suppress.
rationale.P14: Default Synthetic warn_log_rate spike 140 vs 100, auto-resolved in 39s, pat-ai-warn-transient 6% delta. Transient. Suppress.
rationale.P15: LLM 24 Hour Token Usage Warn (75.5% vs 75%): pat-llm-tokens 0% delta, neutral. Cost signal. Suppress.
rationale.P16: [ignore] Default Log Threshold Monitor: 16 fires/hr, self-clears in 20s, 0% delta. Flapper. Suppress.
rationale.P17: Spending Cap Hit Monitor tagged SEV1 (90.8% vs 90% on inference-runner): the SEV1 tag is a misconfigured-rule artifact -- it is a spend_pct budget alert (pat-spend-cap, 0% delta), fired 6x/hr and auto-resolved in 33s, and metrics.csv shows spend_pct sitting at the cap line then dropping to 89.9. Loud label, cost noise underneath. Suppress (precision trap).
rationale.P18: Real inference-runner crashloop: Inference Runner 5xx Rate at inference_500_rate 0.41 vs 0.05, never auto-resolves, metrics.csv shows it climbing (0.004 -> 0.41 -> 0.47 -> 0.49), pat-inference-crashloop surging negative (+870%, CUDA context lost / restart loop) right after the inference-runner-2026.06.21-c deploy. Independent customer-visible incident. Must-page.
rationale.P19: Default Synthetic gpu_mem_pct tagged SEV1 (96% vs 95% on inference-runner): fired 9x/hr, auto-resolved in 28s, pat-ai-gpu-blip 4% delta neutral, metrics.csv shows gpu_mem dropping back to 71. High severity label on a self-cleared warmup blip. Suppress (precision trap).
rationale.P20: Spending Cap Hit Monitor (90.2% on inference-runner): pat-spend-cap 0% delta, neutral. Budget governance. Suppress.

notes: Three independent real incidents: P01 (ai-agent-svc workflow_500 outage, deploy ai-agent-svc-2026.06.21-b), P18 (inference-runner crashloop, deploy inference-runner-2026.06.21-c), and P05 (ai-memory-svc write-failure spike -- quiet SEV3 recall trap, slow sustained rise, no deploy). All three must page. P17 (SEV1-tagged Spending Cap) and P19 (SEV1-tagged GPU blip) are precision traps: loud severity labels over self-resolving cost/blip noise. Remaining alerts are token-usage warns, spend-cap warns, log flappers, CPU blips, and a transient WARN.
